instruction: Write one self-contained program that does ALL of the following, printing results on separate lines: build a map literal of orders with string keys including "theta"; read "data.txt" (lines of "name number") and aggregate the numbers; mix int and float arithmetic (e.g. 3 + 29.5) in a printed result.

orders = {"alpha": 10, "beta": 25, "theta": 7, "gamma": 14}
print "theta order: {orders["theta"]}"
print "total orders: {orders.pairs | map .v | sum}"

lines = fs.read "data.txt" ? "alice 3\nbob 7\ncarol 12"
total = lines.lines | map (ln -> ln.words | last | int) | sum
print "aggregated number total: {total}"

print "total orders: {total_orders}"

lines = fs.read "data.txt" ? "alice 3\nbob 7\ncarol 12"

print "aggregated number total: {total}"

instruction: Write one self-contained program that does ALL of the following, printing results on separate lines: mix int and float arithmetic (e.g. 3 + 84.5) in a printed result; read "data.txt" (lines of "name number") and aggregate the numbers; use match with an err arm on a function that rejects values over 84 with err "limit".
fixed:
check val = if val > 84 { err "limit" } else { val }

lines = fs.read "data.txt" ? "alice 10\nbob 30\ncarol 50"
entries = lines.lines | map line -> {
  parts = line.split " "
  name = parts[0]
  num = parts[1].int
  (name, num)
}

print "--- arithmetic ---"
result = 3 + 84.5
print "3 + 84.5 = {result}"

print "--- aggregation ---"
total = entries | map .1 | sum
print "names: {entries | map .0 | join ', '}"
print "total: {total}"
avg = total.float / entries.len.float
print "average: {avg}"

print "--- match with err ---"
tests = [42, 84, 85, 100, 10]
for val in tests {
  outcome = match (check val) {
    err e -> "rejected {val}: {e}"
    int n -> "accepted {n}"
  }
  print outcome
}
oracle:
--- arithmetic ---
3 + 84.5 = 87.5
--- aggregation ---
names: alice, bob, carol
total: 90
average: 30
--- match with err ---
accepted 42
accepted 84
rejected 85: limit
rejected 100: limit
accepted 10

diag.fix: close the block

stored_diag.fix: close the block

instruction: Write one self-contained program that does ALL of the following, printing results on separate lines: read "data.txt" (lines of "name number") and aggregate the numbers; use one multi-stage pipeline (keep + map at minimum) over a list you define.
fixed:
lines = fs.read "data.txt" ? "alice 10\nbob 20\nalice 5\nbob 15\ncarol 8"

records = lines.lines | map ln -> {
  parts = ln.split " "
  name = parts[0]
  num = parts[1].int ? 0
  {name: name, num: num}
}

for g in records.group .name {
  total = g.v | map .num | sum
  print "{g.k}: {total}"
}

grand = records | map .num | sum
print "total: {grand}"

items = [
  {label: "apple",  score: 3, active: true},
  {label: "banana", score: 7, active: false},
  {label: "cherry", score: 5, active: true},
  {label: "date",   score: 9, active: true},
  {label: "elderberry", score: 2, active: false}
]

result = items | keep .active | map x -> "{x.label}({x.score})" | join ", "
print "active items: {result}"

top_score = items | keep .active | top 1 .score | map .label | join ""
print "top active: {top_score}"
oracle:
alice: 15
bob: 35
carol: 8
total: 58
active items: apple(3), cherry(5), date(9)
top active: date

diag.msg: {label str, score int, active bool} is not callable
diag.fix: only functions can be applied — define `f x = ...` before calling f

stored_diag.msg: {label str, score int, active bool} is not callable
stored_diag.fix: only functions can be applied — define `f x = ...` before calling f

instruction: Write one self-contained program that does ALL of the following, printing results on separate lines: print a missing-key or failed-parse lookup rescued to 3; mix int and float arithmetic (e.g. 3 + 34.5) in a printed result; read "data.txt" (lines of "name number") and aggregate the numbers.
broken:
# Missing-key lookup rescued to 3
m = {"a": 1, "b": 2}
print (m["z"] ? 3)

# Mixed int/float arithmetic
print (3 + 34.5)

# Failed parse rescued to 3
parsed = (int "oops") ? 3
print parsed

# Read data.txt and aggregate
raw = fs.read "data.txt" ? ""
lines = raw.lines | keep x -> x.trim.len > 0
total = lines | fold 0 acc line -> {
  parts = line.split " "
  num = (float parts[1]) ? 0.0
  acc + num
}
print "total: {total}"
for line in lines {
  parts = line.split " "
  name = parts[0]
  num = (float parts[1]) ? 0.0
  print "{name}: {num}"
}
fixed:
# Missing-key lookup rescued to 3
m = {"a": 1, "b": 2}
print (m["z"] ? 3)

# Mixed int/float arithmetic
print (3.float + 34.5)

# Failed parse rescued to 3
parsed = (int "oops") ? 3
print parsed

# Read data.txt and aggregate
raw = fs.read "data.txt" ? ""
lines = raw.lines | keep x -> x.trim.len > 0
total = lines | fold 0.0 acc line -> {
  parts = line.split " "
  num = (float parts[1]) ? 0.0
  acc + num
}
print "total: {total}"
for line in lines {
  parts = line.split " "
  name = parts[0]
  num = (float parts[1]) ? 0.0
  print "{name}: {num}"
}

diag.fix: check the operand types or add a conversion (.int / .float / .str)
